title: Fix avg DPS per voidcore calculation after slot deduplication
description:
Bug reported by user: the avg DPS/voidcore for Vanguard is wrong. User expects ~419.8 avg using Ring + Tier Helm + Catalyzed Belt, but the tool currently uses Ring + Belt + Catalyzed Belt (because of the slot dedup bug) — resulting in an inflated average that double-counts two waist items.

This issue is downstream of drweb-36l (slot dedup fix). Once slot deduplication is implemented, the allSimmedItems map used to compute avgDpsPerVoidcore must also be slot-deduped before averaging. Otherwise the denominator (number of items per pool) will be wrong, and the avg will still be skewed.

Specific fix:
After the slot-dedup pass on targetItems (from drweb-36l), apply the same slot-keyed dedup logic to allSimmedItems: for each targetKey, if two itemIds share the same slot, only keep the one with the higher raw dpsGain in the simmed set.

The avgDpsPerVoidcore formula in lib/voidcore.ts (line ~409-411) is otherwise correct — it sums clamped gains / pool size. The pool size just needs to reflect unique slots.

Acceptance: Vanguard avg/voidcore ≈ 419.8 when the top items are Ring + Tier Helm + Catalyzed Belt, matching user's expected calculation. Verify with raid report https://www.raidbots.com/simbot/report/dvnzvGBqPksaMnmD5cGDcj

title: Deduplicate items by slot per roll target (keep best of base vs catalyst)
description:
Bug reported by user: both the regular and catalyzed versions of the same gear slot (e.g. waist/belt) appear as separate entries in the Voidcore Advisor output. Ideally only the best DPS option per slot should be shown. The same issue occurs in M+ dungeon results.

Root cause (identified in lib/voidcore.ts): The upsert function deduplicates by itemId. A catalyzed tier piece has a *different* itemId than the base item it is crafted from, so both end up in the allItems list for a given target. The base item and its catalyst version occupy the same gear slot but are counted as two separate upgrade opportunities.

Fix needed:
1. After building the itemsMap per targetKey (keyed by itemId), add a second deduplication pass that keeps only the highest dpsGain entry per slot. This ensures that if e.g. itemId 12345 (belt) and itemId 99999 (catalyzed belt) both map to the 'waist' slot for the same target, only the better one is shown.
2. The same slot-dedup logic should apply to allSimmedItems so the avg calculation also only counts one entry per slot per target (see related avg bug).

Note: this dedup should happen AFTER all upserts are done, not inside upsert itself, to keep the logic simple and correct.

The same issue exists in lib/raidbots.ts (main dropr import page) — both regular and catalyst versions appear. Fix there too.

Assuming 'slot' values from the profileset name (parts[6]) correctly identify the gear slot. Verify with raid report https://www.raidbots.com/simbot/report/dvnzvGBqPksaMnmD5cGDcj and m+ report https://www.raidbots.com/simbot/report/gnd1Vgs7aaUMtrLH1pJLYS

title: Fix catalyst tier item missing from vanguard loot table
description:
Bug reported by user: the tier head piece from the Vanguard loot table is not appearing in the Voidcore Advisor output at all.

Root cause (identified in lib/voidcore.ts): itemMap only stores the *first* itemLibrary entry per itemId (line ~157). The catalyst tag and sourceItem.sources are only read for that first entry. If the tier head's catalyst entry appears later in itemLibrary than a non-catalyst entry for the same itemId, catalystSources never gets populated for it — so isCatalyst is false, and the item falls through to the normal-item path which uses item.sources (which for a tier piece likely has instanceId <= 0), causing it to be silently skipped.

Fix: when building catalystSources, iterate ALL entries in itemLibrary (not just the first-seen per id). Specifically, do NOT guard the catalyst detection block with an !itemMap.has(item.id) check. The first-seen guard should only apply to itemMap, not to the catalystSources accumulation.

The same bug exists in lib/raidbots.ts (the dropr import page) and should be fixed there too, though it may be less visible since that page doesn't filter by dungeon/boss.

Acceptance: After fix, the tier head piece from Vanguard appears in the Voidcore Advisor output. Verify with the raid report: https://www.raidbots.com/simbot/report/dvnzvGBqPksaMnmD5cGDcj

title: Fix avgDpsPerVoidcore to use full simmed item pool
description:
## Problem

The current avgDpsPerVoidcore calculation in lib/voidcore.ts (line 379) only averages over items that passed the dpsGain >= 100 filter. This inflates the average because it ignores items that would have been a bad roll outcome.

Example: a boss has 6 simmed items — 3 at +500 DPS, 3 at negative DPS. The current code sees only [500, 500, 500] → avg = 500. The correct expected value per roll is (500+500+500+0+0+0)/6 = 250, since a roll can land on any of the 6 items.

## What needs to change

In parseReport() (lib/voidcore.ts), the code must:

1. Track ALL simmed items per target (boss or dungeon) — both positive and negative gain — not just those that pass the dpsGain >= 100 threshold. Use a separate accumulator (e.g. allSimmedItems) that stores clamped dpsGain values: max(0, dpsGain) per itemId.
2. Compute avgDps as: sum of clamped gains across ALL simmed item slots / total simmed item count.
3. allItems (used for display) should remain filtered to >= 100 dps — only the denominator for the avg changes.
4. avgDpsPerVoidcore = Math.round(avgDps / voidcoreCost) using the corrected avgDps.

## Assumptions

- Assuming 'all simmed items' means every profileset result entry for a given target key, deduped by itemId (keeping max gain per itemId, clamped to 0 from below). This mirrors how the best-gain-per-item dedup already works.
- If the same itemId appears in multiple ilvl variants (which can happen), the highest raw dpsGain is used before clamping — consistent with the existing upsert logic.
- The dpsPerVoidcore field (topItem.dpsGain / voidcoreCost) is unchanged.
- Sorting of targets remains by avgDpsPerVoidcore desc, so the sort order may shift after this fix.

## Acceptance criteria

- A boss with 3 items at +500 and 3 items at -100 DPS yields avgDpsPerVoidcore = Math.round((500+500+500+0+0+0) / 6 / voidcoreCost)
- allItems in the UI still only shows items with dpsGain >= 100
- dpsPerVoidcore (best/voidcore) is unchanged
- npm run build passes with zero TypeScript errors
close_reason: Fixed: allSimmedItems accumulator tracks all profileset results per target (unclamped); avgDpsPerVoidcore now divides sum of clamped gains by total simmed item count. Build passes.
